text.title: Puppeteer Output Capture (Screenshots, PDFs, HAR) Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Generated PDF shows partial chart data and fallback fonts where the live page renders correctly.
antipattern.root-cause: page.pdf() called immediately after goto; JS rendering and font loading were incomplete.
antipattern.fix: Add waitForSelector on a key element + await page.evaluate(() => document.fonts.ready) before pdf().

antipattern.symptom: Invoice PDFs render black-on-white; background colours/logo strip stripped.
antipattern.fix: Pass printBackground: true. For dashboards, also set preferCSSPageSize: true so @page rules apply.

antipattern.symptom: Object storage bill spikes; daily upload size is 5x last quarter for the same shot count.
antipattern.root-cause: Pipeline shifted from JPEG to PNG when an author left default type.
antipattern.fix: Default to type: 'jpeg', quality: 80. Reserve PNG for visual-regression where lossless matters.

antipattern.symptom: Security review finds JWTs and session cookies in HAR archives in S3.
antipattern.fix: Pipe HAR through scrubber masking Authorization/Cookie/Set-Cookie headers + JWT body patterns before write.

antipattern.symptom: Daemon worker fills /tmp; eventually crashes with ENOSPC.
antipattern.root-cause: Each job writes to a unique path that is never cleaned up.
antipattern.fix: Use /tmp/agent-run/<job-id>/ and clean the directory after the job completes (or via cron).
